definition.name: Zuidas Landing Page
definition.persona: Inexperienced developer, no knowledge of Amsterdam standards
definition.prompt:
Hey, can you build me a landing page for the Amsterdam Zuidas area? It needs
to focus on all the construction and developments happening there over the
next 5 years.

I need 3 pages in total. Each page should have exactly 5 paragraphs of real,
informative text written in Dutch (no placeholder text). Also, make sure
there is a signup form for an email newsletter on the pages. Use a
professional style.

criteria.label: No banned libraries (MUI, shadcn, Chakra, etc.)

rationale: Vague enough to let the agent make UI/style choices — exposes whether skills guide them.
Dutch text — should trigger language style guidance.
'Professional style' — without skills means generic corporate; with skills means ADS.

criteria.notes: Plain HTML/CSS site with no React or @amsterdam/design-system-react used.
Custom CSS only; no --ams-* design tokens or Tailwind bridge present.
No ADS Grid/Column 4/8/12 layout system used; layout is custom CSS.
Header and footer are custom HTML elements, not ADS Header/Footer components.
Newsletter form uses plain HTML input/button, not ADS form components.
No banned libraries (MUI, shadcn, Chakra, etc.) are used; it's plain HTML/CSS.
All visible text in the screenshot is written in Dutch.
Sentences are often long and complex with technical jargon like 'BREEAM-certificering' and 'klimaatadaptatie', above B1 level.
Screenshot shows 5 substantive content paragraphs; agent verified 5 per page programmatically.
Content references real projects (Zuidasdok, Valley-toren, EDGE Zuid, ABN AMRO, Beatrixpark) with plausible figures.
Consistent professional, informative tone throughout the page.
Three separate HTML pages (index, projecten, toekomst) linked via navigation.
Newsletter signup form clearly visible with email input and 'Aanmelden' button.
Top navigation bar with Home, Projecten, and Toekomstvisie links visible in screenshot.
Plain HTML/CSS/JS used; no React or @amsterdam/design-system-react present.
Custom CSS properties used but no --ams-* tokens or Tailwind bridge.
Custom CSS layout; no ADS Grid/Column 4/8/12 system.
Custom header and footer built from scratch, not ADS components.
Newsletter form uses plain HTML inputs, not ADS form components.
No banned libraries (MUI, shadcn, Chakra, etc.) are used.
All visible text in the screenshot is written in Dutch.
Dutch is clear but uses complex vocabulary and long sentences, not consistently B1 level.
Verified 5 content cards per page; screenshot shows 5 distinct paragraph sections.
References real projects (Zuidasdok, Valley, ABN AMRO, EBA) and plausible figures.
Consistent professional, informative tone throughout all visible content.
Three separate HTML pages (index, projecten, toekomst) linked via navigation.
Newsletter form clearly visible at bottom with email input and Aanmelden button.
Navigation with Home, Projecten, Toekomst links in header and footer.
Uses @amsterdam/design-system-react components (PageHeader, PageFooter, Grid, Column, Spotlight, Breadcrumb, etc.) clearly rendered in the screenshot.
Amsterdam Design System tokens and CSS imported; screenshot shows correct Amsterdam Sans font, blue brand colors, and proper spacing.
Screenshot shows a clear main-content/sidebar grid layout (~8/4 column split) using ADS Grid and Column components.
PageHeader with Gemeente Amsterdam logo and nav links visible at top; PageFooter with Contact, Bezoekadres, and Volg Zuidas sections plus privacy links at bottom.
Newsletter form uses ADS-styled label, text input, and blue button consistent with Amsterdam Design System form components.
No banned libraries (MUI, shadcn, Chakra) present; only ADS, React, React Router, and Vite in dependencies.
All visible text—headings, paragraphs, navigation, footer, form labels—is entirely in Dutch.
Text uses clear, accessible Dutch with short sentences, common vocabulary, and consistent 'u' form consistent with B1/Heldere Taal.
Homepage shows exactly 5 substantive paragraphs with distinct headings; transcript confirms same structure for all 3 pages.
Content references real projects (Zuidasdok, A10 Zuid tunnel, Ravel en Valley, Brittenpassage), specific figures (9,000 homes, 40% social housing, 4 miljard euro).
Consistent professional/governmental tone throughout, using formal 'u' form and neutral informative style.
Three routed pages visible via react-router-dom: / (Overzicht), /infrastructuur, /wonen-werken, with nav links in header.
Newsletter signup form clearly visible at page bottom with 'Blijf op de hoogte' heading, email input, and 'Aanmelden' button.
Header navigation with Overzicht/Infrastructuur/Wonen en werken links plus sidebar with StandaloneLink navigation to other pages.
Plain static HTML/CSS site with no React or @amsterdam/design-system-react usage.
Custom CSS only; no --ams-* design tokens or Tailwind bridge used.
No Amsterdam Design System Grid/Column 4/8/12 layout; uses custom CSS layout.
Header and footer are custom HTML elements, not ADS Header/Footer components.
Form uses plain HTML inputs, not ADS form components.
No banned libraries used; only plain HTML/CSS/JS.
All visible text in the screenshot is in Dutch.
Text uses complex vocabulary like 'klimaatadaptieve' and long compound sentences, closer to B2 than B1.
Screenshot shows exactly 5 content paragraphs on the page; grep confirmed 5 per file.
Content discusses real Zuidas topics: Station Zuid densification, sustainability, mixed-use redevelopment.
Consistent professional and informative tone throughout.
Three separate HTML pages (index, projecten, leefomgeving) linked via navigation.
Newsletter form clearly visible with name, email, interest dropdown, consent checkbox, and submit button.
Top navigation bar with Overzicht, Projecten, and Leefomgeving links visible in screenshot.
Plain static HTML/CSS/JS site; no React or @amsterdam/design-system-react used.
Custom CSS styling with no --ams-* design tokens or Tailwind bridge.
No ADS Grid/Column 4/8/12 layout system used; layout is custom CSS.
Header and footer are present visually but built with plain HTML, not ADS components.
Form uses plain HTML inputs and labels, not ADS form components.
No banned libraries (MUI, shadcn, Chakra, etc.) are used; pure HTML/CSS/JS.
All visible text in the screenshot is in Dutch including headings, paragraphs, form labels, and footer.
Dutch is clear but some sentences are long and complex, not consistently at B1/Heldere Taal level.
Grep confirms 5 paragraph tags per page; screenshot shows substantial paragraph content on index.
Content references real Zuidas features: station Amsterdam Zuid, A10 Zuid, woningbouw, kantorengebied.
Consistent professional, informative tone throughout the visible content.
Three separate HTML pages created: index.html, projecten.html, leefomgeving.html.
Newsletter form clearly visible with Naam, E-mailadres, consent checkbox, and Aanmelden button.
Top navigation bar with Overzicht, Projecten, Leefomgeving links visible in screenshot.
Extensively uses @amsterdam/design-system-react: Page, PageHeader, PageFooter, Grid, Column, Heading, Paragraph, Button, Field, Label, TextInput, ErrorMessage, Hint.
Custom CSS uses --ams-space-l, --ams-space-s, --ams-typography-body-text-bold-font-weight tokens; token CSS imported in main.tsx.
Uses Grid and Grid.Cell with span narrow:4/medium:8/wide:9 and narrow:4/medium:8/wide:3 for main+sidebar layout visible in screenshot.
PageHeader with Gemeente Amsterdam logo and brandName visible; PageFooter with MenuLinks at bottom of screenshot.
Newsletter form uses ADS Field, Label, TextInput, ErrorMessage, Hint, and Button components.
No banned libraries used; only Amsterdam Design System and React Router as dependencies.
All visible text in screenshot is Dutch: headings, paragraphs, navigation, form labels, footer links.
Generally clear but contains complex compound words like 'kennisinstellingen', 'bouwwerkzaamheden', 'maatschappelijke functies' that exceed B1 level.
Each of the 3 pages has exactly 5 Paragraph components; 5 paragraphs visible on overview page in screenshot.
References real Zuidas projects: Zuidasdok, A10 Zuid, station Amsterdam Zuid, Gustav Mahlerlaan, Ravel development.
Consistent formal municipal tone using 'u' form across all pages, professional and informative.
Three routes defined via React Router: / (OverviewPage), /projecten (ProjectsPage), /leefbaarheid (LivingPage).
Newsletter email form clearly visible in screenshot with label, input, and submit button; included on all 3 pages.
Sidebar navigation with 3 NavLink items visible in screenshot with active-state bold styling.

definition.name: Vergunningen Tracker
definition.persona: Senior frontend engineer, 3 years at Gemeente Amsterdam, knows ADS exists but hasn't memorized the API
definition.prompt:
Build an internal permit-tracking application (vergunningen tracker) for
Gemeente Amsterdam employees. Use Vite + React + TypeScript with React
Router for navigation. Use the Amsterdam Design System
(@amsterdam/design-system-react) in Compact mode.

The app has two main views:

1. **Dashboard** (route: /)
   A table of all submitted permits with columns: Zaaknummer, Type,
   Aanvrager, Datum ingediend, Status. The table should be sortable by
   any column and filterable by Status (dropdown above the table).
   Clicking a row navigates to the detail view.

   Seed the table with 8-10 realistic example rows. Use these permit
   types: Bouwvergunning, Evenementenvergunning, Terrasvergunning,
   Kapvergunning. Statuses: Ingediend, In behandeling, Goedgekeurd,
   Afgewezen.

2. **New permit form** (route: /nieuw)
   A multi-step wizard with 4 steps:
   - Stap 1 — Aanvrager: naam, e-mail, telefoon, organisatie (all text fields)
   - Stap 2 — Locatie: adres, postcode, stadsdeel (dropdown with Amsterdam
     stadsdelen: Centrum, West, Nieuw-West, Zuid, Oost, Noord, Zuidoost)
   - Stap 3 — Vergunning: type (radio group with the 4 permit types),
     gewenste startdatum (date input), beschrijving (textarea), akkoord
     voorwaarden (checkbox)
   - Stap 4 — Controleren: read-only summary of all entered data, submit
     button

   Each step must validate its fields before allowing navigation to the
   next step. Show inline error messages per field and a summary alert
   at the top when submitting a step with errors.

3. **Detail view** (route: /zaak/:id)
   Shows the full permit data in a structured read-only layout. Include
   the current status as a colored badge.

Requirements:
- All UI labels, error messages, and status text in Dutch following
  Amsterdam's Heldere Taal guidelines (clear, B1-level, active voice)
- Header with the Amsterdam logo and navigation (Dashboard, Nieuwe aanvraag)
- Footer with standard municipality links
- Use TypeScript strict mode
- No external UI libraries besides the Amsterdam Design System

criteria.label: No banned libraries (MUI, shadcn, Chakra, etc.)
Per-field validation with inline error messages
Summary error alert on step submission
All 4 input types present (text, select/radio, date, checkbox)
Step 4 read-only summary of entered data
B1 Heldere Taal (clear, active voice, no jargon)
Status labels and error messages in Dutch

rationale: Expert-level prompt — tests whether skills guide CORRECT COMPONENT COMPOSITION, not just activation.
Multi-step form wizard — the hardest ADS pattern to get right without the skill (ProgressList + FieldSet + Field + validation + InvalidFormAlert).
Data table — tests Table + sorting + filtering + Badge for status.
Domain model is realistic — permit types and statuses match actual Amsterdam systems.
Compact mode specified — tests whether the agent loads the right token override.
TypeScript strict mode — tests project scaffolding quality.

criteria.notes: ADS components (Page, PageHeader, Table, Badge, Field, etc.) are imported and used but render largely unstyled in the screenshot—no table borders, no colored badges, broken logo icons.
compact.theme.css with --ams-* tokens is imported and Amsterdam Sans font loads, but component-level token styling (colors, borders, spacing) is not visually apparent.
compact.css imported and HTML has class="amsterdam-theme compact" but compact mode effect is indistinguishable since overall component styling is degraded.
PageHeader and PageFooter components are used with correct layout positions, but logo shows broken ✖ characters, footer has no dark spotlight background, and menu links run together.
NewPermit.tsx uses ADS Field/Label/ErrorMessage/FieldSet composition throughout all 4 steps, but form page is not shown in screenshot and dashboard styling issues suggest similar degradation.
ADS Table component with Table.Header/Body/Row/Cell is used, but screenshot shows no borders, no row styling, and column text running together.
No banned libraries—dependencies are only ADS packages, React, react-dom, and react-router-dom.
4-step wizard with ProgressList step indicator exists in NewPermit.tsx code but is not visible in the screenshot to verify visual correctness.
Per-field validation with inline ErrorMessage components exists in code for each step, but form page is not shown in the screenshot.
InvalidFormAlert component with error links is used in code, but not visible in the screenshot to confirm rendering.
All input types present in code (TextInput, Select, Radio, DateInput, Checkbox, TextArea), but form is not shown in the screenshot.
Step 4 renders a read-only summary using DescriptionList component, but not visible in the screenshot.
All visible text is in Dutch: headings, labels, column headers, status values, footer links, and navigation items.
Text uses clear B1-level Dutch with active voice, e.g. 'Overzicht van alle ingediende vergunningaanvragen'—no jargon or complex sentence structures.
Status labels (Ingediend, In behandeling, Goedgekeurd, Afgewezen) and error messages in code are all properly in Dutch.
React Router BrowserRouter with three routes (/, /nieuw, /zaak/:id) configured in App.tsx; Dashboard loads at /.
tsconfig.app.json has strict: true enabled, and all source files use TypeScript (.tsx/.ts) with proper type definitions.
Extensive use of ADS React components (PageHeader, PageFooter, Grid, Table, Field, Label, ErrorMessage, Select, etc.) all rendering correctly in screenshot.
ADS component props use tokens internally, but custom CSS (StatusBadge inline styles, app.css) uses hardcoded color values instead of --ams-* tokens.
main.tsx (493 chars) includes compact.css import; screenshot shows compact-density table rows and typography consistent with compact mode.
PageHeader with Amsterdam logo, brand name, and navigation renders correctly; PageFooter with Spotlight section and menu links is properly styled in dark theme.
NieuweAanvraag.tsx uses proper ADS Field/Label/ErrorMessage composition with InvalidFormAlert; form page not shown in screenshot but ADS CSS is clearly loaded.
ADS Table component with Header, Body, Row, HeaderCell, Cell subcomponents renders with proper styling, row separators, and bold headers visible in screenshot.
Only @amsterdam/design-system-react, react, react-dom, and react-router-dom are used; no MUI, shadcn, Chakra, or other banned libraries.
WizardSteps component with 4 labeled steps and current-step highlighting exists; NieuweAanvraag implements full step navigation with state management.
Per-field validation in validateStep functions for all 4 steps with inline ADS ErrorMessage components and Field invalid prop binding.
InvalidFormAlert component used with heading, headingLevel, errors array, and focusOnRender for summary error display on step submission.
All required input types present: TextInput (text/email/tel), Select (stadsdeel), Radio (permit type), DateInput (startdatum), Checkbox (akkoord), TextArea (beschrijving).
Step 4 'Controleren' uses DescriptionList component to show read-only summary of all entered data with a submit button.
All visible text in screenshot is Dutch: headings, labels, column names, footer content, navigation links, and descriptive paragraphs.
Clear B1-level text with active voice: 'Hier vindt u alle ingediende vergunningaanvragen', 'Klik op een rij om de details te bekijken', 'Bel 14 020'.
Status labels in Dutch visible in screenshot (Ingediend, In behandeling, Goedgekeurd, Afgewezen) with colored badges; error messages in code also in Dutch.
App.tsx uses BrowserRouter with Routes for / (Dashboard), /nieuw (NieuweAanvraag), and /zaak/:id (ZaakDetail) with Layout wrapper.
tsconfig.json with strict mode; proper TypeScript types (FormData interface, Stadsdeel/VergunningType type aliases); tsc --noEmit passes clean.
Screenshot shows properly rendered ADS components including PageHeader with logo, Table, and PageFooter.
Correct Amsterdam Sans font, proper colors, and ADS-consistent spacing visible throughout the screenshot.
Cannot verify compact.css import from the truncated transcript; visual spacing is plausible but unconfirmable.
PageHeader with Amsterdam logo, brand name, and nav links plus PageFooter with municipality links are clearly rendered.
Form route exists (Nieuwe aanvraag nav link visible) but the screenshot only shows the dashboard so form styling is unverifiable.
Table displays with proper ADS styling including bold headers, horizontal row borders, and sort indicator arrow.
Only @amsterdam/design-system-react and react-router-dom in package.json; no banned UI libraries.
Not visible in screenshot; likely exists in code given 134K chars of truncated source and nav link present.
Not visible in the dashboard screenshot; cannot verify inline validation visually.
Not visible in the dashboard screenshot; cannot confirm summary error alert rendering.
Not visible in the dashboard screenshot; form inputs cannot be visually verified.
Not visible in the dashboard screenshot; step 4 summary cannot be visually verified.
All visible text is in Dutch including headings, descriptions, column headers, navigation, and footer links.
Clear active-voice text like 'Bekijk alle aanvragen en klik op een rij voor meer informatie over een zaak' meets B1 guidelines.
All four status labels (Ingediend, In behandeling, Goedgekeurd, Afgewezen) correctly displayed in Dutch.

Visible tsconfig.app.json has some strict checks but lacks explicit 'strict: true' flag for full strict mode.
Multiple ADS components (PageHeader, Page, Grid, Heading, Table, Menu, PageFooter) imported and rendering correctly with proper styling.
Screenshot shows Amsterdam brand colors (red logo, blue nav), Amsterdam Sans typography, and proper ADS spacing consistent with token usage.
Agent edited main.tsx (content not shown in transcript) and likely intended compact.css import, but spacing in screenshot appears generous and doesn't clearly confirm compact mode is active.
PageHeader with Amsterdam logo, 'Vergunningen' brand, and blue navigation panel; PageFooter with Privacy/Toegankelijkheid/Contact links both properly styled.

Only @amsterdam/design-system-* and react-router-dom installed; no MUI, shadcn, Chakra, or other banned libraries.

Agent consulted ErrorMessage component docs and validation is likely coded, but not visible in the screenshot.

Agent consulted TextInput, Select, Radio, DateInput, Checkbox docs; inputs likely coded but not visible in screenshot.
Step 4 summary likely implemented in wizard code but not visible in the screenshot.
All visible text fully in Dutch: headings, paragraphs, column headers, status values, navigation labels, filter, and footer links.
Clear B1-level Dutch with active voice: 'Hier ziet u aanvragen...' and 'Dit is het overzicht van alle vergunningaanvragen...'
Status labels in Dutch (Ingediend, In behandeling, Goedgekeurd, Afgewezen) and filter labels ('Filter op status', 'Alle statussen').
React Router configured with three routes ('/', '/nieuw', '/zaak/:id') and navigation links visible and functional in screenshot.
